title: securigera varia subsp. orientalis
intro: Securigera varia subsp. orientalis, also known as the hairy clover or Oriental knapweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's health benefits include soothing coughs and colds, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable component in traditional herbal remedies. Securigera varia subsp. orientalis is often prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
